symptoms.symptom_name: Prolonged low-grade fever with chills and drenching night sweats (two weeks)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Persistent fever is the cardinal feature of endocarditis and a Duke minor criterion.
A two-week unremitting febrile course is atypical for a self-limiting viral illness.

symptoms.symptom_name: Painful tender red nodules on finger pads (Osler's nodes)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Osler's nodes are a classic immune/embolic peripheral stigma of endocarditis (Duke minor criterion, vascular/immunological phenomena); patient-visible on video so admissible in telehealth.
Splinter haemorrhages are an embolic peripheral sign supporting endocarditis; video-observable so admissible in telehealth.
A predisposing structural valve lesion is a Duke minor criterion and the substrate on which vegetations form.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound fatigue, myalgia and ~3 kg unintentional weight loss

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Constitutional decline reflects chronic bacteraemia; non-specific but consistent with a subacute endovascular infection.
B-symptoms could suggest malignancy, but the embolic stigmata and valvular murmur redirect toward endocarditis.

symptoms.symptom_name: New early diastolic aortic regurgitation murmur

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A new regurgitant murmur is a Duke MAJOR criterion — sealed clinician auscultation finding; the AI infers valve involvement from history and stigmata, not from a heard murmur.
Splenomegaly reflects chronic bacteraemia/immune stimulation — sealed clinician palpation finding, not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Vital signs — HR 102, BP 110/65, RR 18, SpO2 97%, temperature 38.4 C

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tachycardia and low-grade pyrexia support systemic infection; measured BP/HR/RR/SpO2 are sealed clinician observations — the AI has only the patient's home thermometer reading and video appearance.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive blood cultures for a typical endocarditis organism

investigation_details.expected_result: Growth of a typical endocarditis organism (e.g. viridans streptococci, Staphylococcus aureus or enterococcus)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without cultures the organism and sensitivities are unknown and directed IV therapy cannot be chosen; premature antibiotics render cultures negative.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Typical-organism bacteraemia is a Duke MAJOR criterion — a hospital investigation, never available in real time via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Valvular vegetation with new aortic regurgitation on transoesophageal echocardiogram

investigation_details.expected_result: Aortic-valve vegetation on the bicuspid valve with significant aortic regurgitation
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Vegetation size, valve destruction and embolic risk remain unquantified, delaying surgical decision-making.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Echocardiographic vegetation is a Duke MAJOR criterion — a hospital investigation, not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Denies chest pain, haematuria and focal neurological deficit

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of current embolic complications (stroke, renal infarct) does not lower endocarditis probability but defines the baseline against which deterioration is monitored.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Classic peripheral endocarditis stigmata on a predisposing valve
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Prolonged fever plus Osler's nodes plus splinter haemorrhages in a patient with a bicuspid aortic valve is a high-probability endocarditis picture that mandates emergency hospital work-up.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Multiple blood culture sets before any antibiotics
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Organism identification is essential for directed IV therapy and is destroyed by premature community antibiotics — cultures must precede treatment.
Confirms vegetation, quantifies valve destruction and regurgitation, and guides surgical decision-making.
